title: ggsvelte PortableSpec
description: A ggsvelte plot specification (schema v0 — UNSTABLE, pre-0.1.0; served from the repo until schema hosting exists).

CellValue.description: A single data cell: string, number, boolean, or null. Dates travel as ISO 8601 strings (e.g. "2026-07-10"); non-finite numbers travel as null.

values.description: Row-oriented inline data: an array of row objects sharing field names.

items.description: One data row: an object mapping field names to cell values.

columns.description: Column-oriented inline data: an object mapping each field name to an array of cell values. All arrays must have the same length.

DataName.description: A reference to a named dataset. Example: {"name": "cars"}.

name.description: Name of a dataset provided out-of-band: either a key of the spec's top-level `datasets`, or a dataset passed by the host at render time.

DataRef.description: Where a plot's data comes from: inline rows ({values}), inline columns ({columns}), or a named dataset ({name}). Exactly one form.

InlineData.description: Inline data only ({values} or {columns}); used for entries of `datasets`.

FieldRef.description: Map this channel to a data field (column). Example: {"field": "displ"}. This is the canonical form — bare strings are NOT valid channel values.

field.description: Name of the data column this channel reads from.

ValueRef.description: Map this channel to a constant. Example: {"value": "steelblue"} sets a literal color; {"value": "treatment", "scale": true} routes the constant through the scale.

value.description: The constant value applied to every mark.

scale.description: If true, the constant is passed THROUGH the channel's scale (like a data value). Default false: the constant is used literally (e.g. a CSS color).

StatRef.description: Map this channel to a column generated by the layer's stat (after-stat form). Example: {"stat": "count"}.

stat.description: Name of a stat-generated column computed after the layer's stat runs (e.g. "count" for the count stat). ggplot2's after_stat().

ChannelValue.description: How one aesthetic channel gets its value: a data field ({field}), a constant ({value, scale?}), a stat output ({stat}), or null to UNSET a channel inherited from the plot-level mapping. Bare strings are not valid.

Aes.description: Aesthetic mapping: which channels read which data fields/constants. Plot-level aes is inherited by every layer; a layer's aes overrides per channel, and null unsets an inherited channel.

color.description: Stroke/point color channel. Discrete fields get a categorical palette; quantitative fields get a sequential ramp.

fill.description: Fill color channel (areas, bars, filled shapes). Discrete fields get a categorical palette; quantitative fields get a sequential ramp.

size.description: Mark size channel (AREA-scaled — sqrt scale; use `linewidth` for stroke width, which scales linearly).

linewidth.description: Stroke width channel (LENGTH-scaled — linear; distinct from `size`, which scales by area).

group.description: Explicit grouping channel. Overrides the default grouping (the interaction of all discrete mapped aesthetics). A constant (e.g. {"value": 1}) forces a single group.

label.description: Text label channel (used by the text geom; never participates in grouping).

weight.description: Statistical weight channel. The count, bin, and density stats sum weights instead of counting rows. Never participates in grouping.

ymin.description: Lower bound channel (errorbar with the identity stat). Quantitative values only.

ymax.description: Upper bound channel (errorbar with the identity stat). Quantitative values only.

PointParams.description: Styling parameters for the point geom.

alpha.description: Point opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

size.description: Point radius in px. Must be greater than 0. Default 2.5.

LineParams.description: Styling parameters for the line geom.

alpha.description: Line opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

linewidth.description: Stroke width in px. Must be greater than 0. Default 1.5.

curve.description: Interpolation between points: "linear" (straight segments, default) or "step" (horizontal-then-vertical steps, changing at the midpoint between x positions).

ColParams.description: Styling parameters for the col geom.

alpha.description: Bar opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

width.description: Bar width as a fraction of the band step. Must be greater than 0 and at most 1. Default 0.9.

BarParams.description: Parameters for the bar and histogram geoms: styling (alpha, width) plus stat-bin binning controls (bins, binwidth, boundary, center, closed).

alpha.description: Bar opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

width.description: Bar width as a fraction of the band step (count-stat bars only; binned bars span their bin). Must be greater than 0 and at most 1. Default 0.9.

bins.description: STAT BIN ONLY: number of bins (an integer of at least 1). Default 30 — an advisory reminds you to pick a real value. Overridden by binwidth.

binwidth.description: STAT BIN ONLY: bin width in data units (must be greater than 0). Takes precedence over bins. Usually the better knob: pick a width meaningful for the data.

boundary.description: STAT BIN ONLY: align a bin EDGE with this x value (e.g. 0 puts bin edges at multiples of the width). Mutually exclusive with center.

center.description: STAT BIN ONLY: align a bin CENTER with this x value. Mutually exclusive with boundary.

closed.description: STAT BIN ONLY: which edge of each bin is inclusive: "right" (default, matches ggplot2) or "left".

SmoothParams.description: Parameters for the smooth geom (fitted trend line + optional se ribbon).

method.description: Smoothing method: "lm" (least-squares line) or "loess" (local polynomial regression). Omit to infer: loess for fewer than 1000 rows, lm above (an advisory reports the choice).

se.description: Draw the confidence-interval ribbon around the fit. Default true.

level.description: Confidence level of the ribbon, strictly between 0 and 1. Default 0.95.

span.description: LOESS ONLY: fraction of points in each local neighborhood, greater than 0 and at most 1. Default 0.75. Smaller = wigglier.

degree.description: LOESS ONLY: degree of the local polynomial, 1 or 2. Default 2 (the R default).

n.description: Number of evaluation points along x (an integer between 2 and 5000). Default 80.

linewidth.description: Stroke width of the fitted line in px. Must be greater than 0. Default 1.

alpha.description: Line opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

BoxplotParams.description: Styling parameters for the boxplot geom.

width.description: Box width as a fraction of the band step. Must be greater than 0 and at most 1. Default 0.9.

coef.description: Whisker length as a multiple of the IQR (values beyond it are outliers). Must be at least 0. Default 1.5.

linewidth.description: Stroke width of the box, whiskers, and median line in px (the median draws at twice this). Must be greater than 0. Default 1.

outlierSize.description: Outlier point radius in px. Must be greater than 0. Default 1.5.

alpha.description: Layer opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

DensityParams.description: Parameters for the density geom (gaussian kernel density estimate).

bw.description: Kernel bandwidth in data units (must be greater than 0). Omit for R's bw.nrd0 rule-of-thumb default.

adjust.description: Bandwidth multiplier (must be greater than 0). Default 1. 0.5 = half the default bandwidth (wigglier), 2 = double (smoother).

n.description: Number of grid points the density is evaluated at (an integer between 2 and 4096). Default 512 (the R default).

cut.description: The grid extends cut * bandwidth beyond the data extremes (at least 0). Default 3 (the R default).

alpha.description: Area fill opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

ErrorbarParams.description: Parameters for the errorbar geom: styling plus the summary-stat functions (fun, funMin, funMax) used when stat is "summary".

width.description: Cap width as a fraction of the band step. Must be greater than 0 and at most 1. Default 0.9.

linewidth.description: Stroke width in px. Must be greater than 0. Default 1.

alpha.description: Errorbar opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

fun.description: STAT SUMMARY ONLY: the center summary of y per x group: "mean" (default), "median", or "sum". With "mean" and no funMin/funMax, the bounds default to mean ± standard error (ggplot2's mean_se).

funMin.description: STAT SUMMARY ONLY: summary function for the lower bound (ymin). Overrides the mean_se default.

funMax.description: STAT SUMMARY ONLY: summary function for the upper bound (ymax). Overrides the mean_se default.

PositionParams.description: Parameters for the jitter (width, height, seed) and nudge (x, y) position adjustments.

width.description: JITTER ONLY: maximum horizontal jitter amount — data units on continuous x, band-step fractions on discrete x. Default 40% of the data resolution.

height.description: JITTER ONLY: maximum vertical jitter amount — data units on continuous y, band-step fractions on discrete y. Default 40% of the data resolution.

seed.description: JITTER ONLY: RNG seed (a non-negative integer). Default 42. ggsvelte jitter is ALWAYS seeded so renders are reproducible (deliberate divergence from ggplot2's random jitter).

x.description: NUDGE ONLY: horizontal offset — data units on continuous x, band-step fractions on discrete x. Default 0.

y.description: NUDGE ONLY: vertical offset — data units on continuous y, band-step fractions on discrete y. Default 0.

AreaParams.description: Styling parameters for the area geom.

alpha.description: Area fill opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

RuleIntercept.description: A fixed intercept (or array of intercepts) in data units. Numbers for linear/log scales; ISO 8601 date strings (e.g. "2026-01-01") for time scales; category strings for band scales.

RuleParams.description: Styling parameters for the rule geom. The annotation form sets xintercept and/or yintercept here; the data-driven form maps aes.x OR aes.y instead (never both forms at once).

xintercept.description: ANNOTATION FORM ONLY: draw a vertical rule at each of these fixed x positions. Mutually exclusive with mapping aes.x/aes.y on this layer.

yintercept.description: ANNOTATION FORM ONLY: draw a horizontal rule at each of these fixed y positions. Mutually exclusive with mapping aes.x/aes.y on this layer.

alpha.description: Rule opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

linewidth.description: Stroke width in px. Must be greater than 0. Default 1.

TextParams.description: Styling parameters for the text geom (no collision detection: labels draw exactly where placed).

alpha.description: Text opacity. Must be between 0 and 1 (inclusive). Default 1.

size.description: Font size in px. Must be greater than 0. Default 11.

anchor.description: Horizontal text anchor relative to the (x, y) position: "start", "middle" (default), or "end".

dx.description: Horizontal offset in px applied after positioning. Default 0.

dy.description: Vertical offset in px applied after positioning (positive = down). Default 0.

RenderBackend.description: Rendering backend for this layer: "svg" (DOM marks — accessible, copyable), "canvas" (fast raster for high mark counts), or "auto" (default — canvas above the mark-count threshold of 2000, with an advisory). Text layers always render as SVG. renderToSVGString ignores this and renders everything as SVG.

StackablePosition.description: Position adjustment: "stack" piles grouped values (positive up, negative down), "fill" stacks to proportions of 1, "dodge" places groups side by side, "identity" leaves positions unchanged.

PointLayer.description: A scatter/point layer. Requires x and y channels (inherited from plot aes or set in the layer's aes).

geom.description: Point geometry: one mark per data row. Use for scatter plots, dot plots, bubbles, correlation views.

stat.description: Point layers draw the data as-is.

position.description: Position adjustment: "identity" (default), "jitter" (seeded random offsets — configure with positionParams.width/height/seed), or "nudge" (fixed offsets — positionParams.x/y).

LineLayer.description: A line layer. Requires x and y channels; rows are sorted by x within each group before connecting.

geom.description: Line geometry: connects points in x order, one line per group (groups derive from discrete aesthetics such as color, or from aes.group). Use for time series, trends, line charts.

stat.description: Line layers draw the data as-is.

ColLayer.description: A column (pre-computed bar) layer. Requires x (discrete) and y (the bar height) channels. Default position "stack".

geom.description: Column geometry: one rectangle per data row, from the y baseline (zero) to the row's y value. Use when the data already contains the bar heights (ggplot2's geom_col).

stat.description: Col layers draw the given y values as-is.

BarLayer.description: A counting/binning bar layer. Requires the x channel; y is computed by the stat. Default position "stack". Use geom "col" when the data already contains the heights.

geom.description: Bar geometry with counting or binning: one rectangle per distinct x value (stat count, discrete x) or per bin (stat bin, continuous x). Do NOT map aes.y — the stat computes it (ggplot2's geom_bar / geom_histogram).

stat.description: The bar layer's stat: "count" (default — rows counted per distinct x value and group) or "bin" (continuous x binned; the canonical form of the histogram geom). Map aes.weight to sum weights instead of counting. y defaults to {"stat": "count"}.

HistogramLayer.description: A histogram layer (alias for bar + stat bin). Requires a continuous x channel; y is computed by the bin stat. Default position "stack". Set params.binwidth or params.bins (default 30, with an advisory).

geom.description: Histogram geometry: a continuous x variable divided into bins, one bar per bin whose height is the count of rows (or the sum of aes.weight). Do NOT map aes.y — the bin stat computes it. Canonicalized by normalize() to a bar layer with stat bin.

stat.description: Histogram layers bin continuous x values. y defaults to {"stat": "count"}; set y to {"stat": "density"} for a normalized histogram.

SmoothLayer.description: A fitted-trend layer. Requires quantitative x and y channels. Usually layered over a point layer of the same data.

geom.description: Smooth geometry: a fitted trend line (with an optional confidence ribbon) over an x/y scatter, one fit per group. Use to reveal trends (ggplot2's geom_smooth).

stat.description: Smooth layers fit lm or loess per group and evaluate the fit at params.n points.

BoxplotLayer.description: A boxplot layer. Requires a discrete x channel and a quantitative y channel.

geom.description: Box-and-whisker geometry: one box per x category (per group) summarizing the y distribution — hinges at the quartiles, whiskers to the furthest points within coef × IQR, outliers drawn individually.

stat.description: Boxplot layers compute five-number summaries (type-7 quantiles, the R default) per group.

position.description: Position adjustment: "dodge" (default — grouped boxes sit side by side within each x band) or "identity".

DensityLayer.description: A kernel-density layer. Requires a continuous x channel; y is computed by the density stat. Map fill (with alpha) for overlaid group comparisons.

geom.description: Density geometry: a smooth kernel density estimate of a continuous x variable, drawn as a filled area — a smoothed histogram alternative. One curve per group. Do NOT map aes.y — the density stat computes it.

stat.description: Density layers run a gaussian KDE per group (bandwidth: R's bw.nrd0 unless params.bw is set). y defaults to {"stat": "density"}; set y to {"stat": "count"} for count scaling.

ErrorbarLayer.description: An errorbar layer. Identity stat: requires x, ymin, and ymax channels. Summary stat: requires x and y channels (bounds computed by params.fun/funMin/funMax, default mean_se).

geom.description: Errorbar geometry: a vertical range with caps at ymin and ymax, one per data row (identity stat) or per x group (summary stat).

stat.description: The errorbar's stat: "identity" (default — map aes.ymin and aes.ymax to data fields) or "summary" (compute y/ymin/ymax per x group from aes.y; default mean ± standard error, ggplot2's mean_se).

AreaLayer.description: An area layer. Requires x and y channels; rows are sorted by x within each group. Default position "stack".

geom.description: Area geometry: a filled region from the y baseline (zero) to the y value, connected in x order per group. Use for stacked composition-over-time charts.

stat.description: Area layers draw the data as-is.

RuleLayer.description: A reference-line layer (ggplot2's geom_vline/geom_hline, unified). Annotation form: fixed intercepts in params. Data-driven form: map aes.x OR aes.y.

geom.description: Rule geometry: reference lines spanning the panel. TWO HONEST FORMS: (1) annotation — set params.xintercept and/or params.yintercept to fixed data values and map neither aes.x nor aes.y; (2) data-driven — map exactly ONE of aes.x (vertical rules) or aes.y (horizontal rules) to a field. Never mix the forms in one layer.

stat.description: Rule layers draw the given positions as-is.

TextLayer.description: A text-label layer. Requires x, y, and label channels.

geom.description: Text geometry: one label per data row at (x, y). No collision detection — labels draw exactly where placed. Requires x, y, and label channels.

stat.description: Text layers draw the data as-is.

position.description: Position adjustment: "identity" (default) or "nudge" (fixed offsets from the anchor — set positionParams.x/y; useful for labels beside marks).

LayerSpec.description: One plot layer. The `geom` field selects the geometry and determines which stat, position, and params are allowed.

DomainValue.description: One domain entry: a number (linear/log), an ISO 8601 date string (time), or a category value (band/ordinal).

PositionScaleSpec.description: Configuration for a positional (x or y) scale.

type.description: Scale type: "linear" (default for numbers), "log" (base 10; the whole domain must be positive), "time" (temporal fields; ISO strings or Date values), "band" (discrete categories). Omit to infer from the field type.

domain.description: Explicit domain, PINNING the scale: [min, max] for continuous scales (numbers, or ISO date strings for time); the full category list for band scales. Data outside the domain is dropped with a warning.

nice.description: Round the inferred domain to tick-friendly bounds. Default true. Ignored when `domain` is set.

zero.description: Force the domain to include zero. Bars, columns, and areas force this to true on the measure axis; set false explicitly to override. Ignored when `domain` is set.

reverse.description: Reverse the scale's output direction. Default false.

breaks.description: Explicit tick positions in data units (numbers, or ISO date strings for time scales). Omit for automatic ticks.

labels.description: Tick label format string. Time scales: strftime-style ("%Y-%m", "%b %d", "%H:%M"). Numeric scales: ",d" (grouped integer), ".1f" (fixed decimals), ".0%" (percent), "~s" (SI prefix). Omit for automatic formatting.

ColorScaleSpec.description: Configuration for a color or fill scale.

type.description: Color scale type: "ordinal" (discrete categories, default for discrete fields) or "sequential" (continuous ramp, default for quantitative fields).

domain.description: Explicit domain, PINNING the scale: the category list for ordinal scales (values outside it render the unknown color); [min, max] numbers for sequential scales.

domainMode.description: Ordinal domain stability: "grow" (default — first-seen order, assignments keyed by value; removing a series never recolors the others) or "data" (legacy rebuild-per-render). Ignored when `domain` is set.

range.description: Explicit output colors (CSS color strings). Ordinal: the palette, in domain order. Sequential: ramp stops, evenly spaced.

scheme.description: Named color scheme: "observable10" (categorical, default for ordinal) or "viridis" (perceptually uniform ramp, default for sequential).

reverse.description: Reverse the color range. Default false.

onExhaust.description: What happens when an ordinal scale runs out of palette entries: "cycle" (default — reuse colors and warn once) or "error" (fail the render; use for correctness-critical charts).

labels.description: Legend label format string for sequential scales (numeric formats like ".1f", ",d", ".0%"). Omit for automatic formatting.

Scales.description: Per-scale configuration, keyed by aesthetic. Omitted scales use inference (type from field data, domain from data extent).

LegendSpec.description: Legend options. Legends style only through the theme.

order.description: Order of discrete legend entries: "stable-domain" (default — stored assignment order, stable across data changes), "present-first-seen" (first occurrence in the current data), "sorted" (alphabetical). Ordering NEVER changes color assignments.

ThemeName.description: A registered theme name: "default" (inherits the page color via currentColor), "light", "dark", or "minimal".

ThemeSpec.description: A theme object: a named base plus role overrides. Roles feed geom defaults (ink/paper/accent); every color rides a --gg-* CSS custom property so hosts can restyle without a re-render.

ink.description: Foreground role (CSS color): axis lines, tick labels, titles, unmapped line/point/text marks.

paper.description: Background role (CSS color) painted behind the plot. "none" for transparent.

accent.description: Accent role (CSS color): default fill for unmapped bars, columns, and areas.

Labs.description: Human-readable labels: titles, axis titles, legend titles, caption.

x.description: X axis title. Defaults to the mapped field name.

y.description: Y axis title. Defaults to the mapped field name.

color.description: Color legend title. Defaults to the mapped field name.

fill.description: Fill legend title. Defaults to the mapped field name.

FacetScales.description: How positional scales behave across facet panels: "fixed" (default — all panels share both scales, trained on the union of panel data), "free" (both positional scales train per panel), "free_x" (only x is per-panel), "free_y" (only y is per-panel). Discrete color/fill assignments are ALWAYS global (one legend), regardless of this setting.

FacetSpec.description: Facet the plot into small-multiple panels. Wrap form: set `wrap` (+ optional ncol). Grid form: set `rows` and/or `cols`. Panels partition the data BEFORE stats and positions run (each panel computes its own counts, bins, stacks). Panel values sort ascending; null values form their own panel.

wrap.description: Facet WRAP form: partition rows by this data field's distinct values, one panel per value, wrapped into a grid ncol wide (ggplot2's facet_wrap). Mutually exclusive with rows/cols.

rows.description: Facet GRID form: the field whose distinct values become grid rows (ggplot2's facet_grid rows). Combine with cols; mutually exclusive with wrap.

cols.description: Facet GRID form: the field whose distinct values become grid columns (ggplot2's facet_grid cols). Combine with rows; mutually exclusive with wrap.

ncol.description: WRAP ONLY: number of panel columns (an integer of at least 1). Omit for an automatic near-square layout (ceil(sqrt(panels))).

CoordSpec.description: The plot's coordinate system. {"type": "flip"} turns any vertical composition into its horizontal counterpart (ggplot2's coord_flip): map x to the category and y to the value as usual, then flip.

type.description: Coordinate system: "cartesian" (default) or "flip" (swap the axes: x renders vertically, y horizontally — THE mechanism for horizontal bar charts; positions, stacking, dodging, and hit-testing all follow).

PlotSpec.description: A complete ggsvelte plot specification: data + aesthetic mapping + one or more layers, in ggplot2's layered grammar. Strictly JSON (PortableSpec).

$schema.description: URL of the ggsvelte spec JSON Schema version this spec targets.

edition.description: Defaults edition this spec was authored against (currently 1). normalize() stamps the current edition when absent, so a spec keeps ITS edition's default look (theme roles, categorical palette) even after ggsvelte's defaults improve in a later edition. Explicit theme/scale settings always win over edition defaults.

datasets.description: Named inline datasets referenced by {"name": ...} data refs.

aes.description: Plot-level aesthetic mapping, inherited by every layer.

layers.description: The plot's layers, drawn in array order (first = bottom). At least one layer.

theme.description: A registered theme name, or a theme object (named base + role overrides). Omit for the default theme.

width.description: Preferred plot width in px (greater than 0). The host may override.

height.description: Preferred plot height in px (greater than 0). The host may override.

a11y.description: Accessibility mode: "auto" (default — layers may render to canvas per their `render` hint, paired with an off-screen description block) or "force-svg" (every layer renders as SVG DOM marks — use for assistive-technology-critical charts; canvas layers do not expose per-mark accessibility).
